items.title: Man denied assisted dying wins partial victory in early test of ACT law

children.content: A tribunal has ruled on a man's bid to overturn his rejection from the ACT's assisted dying scheme in one of the first tests of how people can challenge such decisions.

image.altText: A building sign that reads 'ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal'.

items.title: Former ACT politician to stand trial on grooming charges

children.content: Former ACT Attorney-General Gordon Ramsay is committed to stand trial for allegedly grooming a teenage boy between 2022 and 2025.

image.altText: Gordon Ramsay walks outside the court in a suit and tie.

items.title: NSW scrapped this 'frustrating' road rule. Should the ACT follow?

children.content: The NRMA is encouraging all states and territories to follow the lead of NSW in removing reduced speed limits for roadworks outside construction hours.

gallery.altText: A temporary sign saying Roadwork Ahead and a 40 kph speed limit.

thumbnail.altText: A temporary sign saying Roadwork Ahead and a 40 kph speed limit.

items.title: Jewish ANU student called 'baby killer' on campus, royal commission hears

children.content: The Royal Commission into Anti Semitism and Social Cohesion has heard universities need to improve responses when Jewish staff and students feel unsafe.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair in a dark blazer and white shirt stands behind a microphone looking off camera.

items.title: ACT signs deal for CSIRO site, plans to build 3,000 homes

children.content: After being flagged for development over a decade ago, the ACT government has reached an agreement for the sale of land at a former CSIRO agriculture research site to construction of thousands new homes.

image.altText: A drone shot of green pastures

items.title: As the mercury drops in Canberra, cyclists are still braving the chill

children.content: Every year, winter sees many Canberrans hanging up their helmets, despite the city being considered Australia's "cycling capital." This year though, cyclist numbers have remained relatively steady, even as the mercury drops.

gallery.altText: A man rides along on a cargo-bike.

thumbnail.altText: A man rides along on a cargo-bike.

items.title: 'He gave us 10 extra years': Australian-first immune centre opens in Canberra

children.content: The Centre for Personalised Medicine at Canberra Hospital uses cutting-edge technology to identify the causes of complex immune conditions to tailor treatments for patients.

image.altText: An elderly man and his adult daughter smiling outside.

items.title: First Indigenous Military Cross recipient discovered by War Memorial

children.content: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

image.altText: A Military Cross medal in front of a line of other military medals.

items.title: Man shot dead in Sydney's north-west in 'very brazen attack'

children.content: A man has been shot dead in Sydney's north-west, with the homicide squad now leading the investigation.

image.altText: Three officers, police tape and a white car at the crime scene.

items.title: Quick hits: Spain's secret to success, Yamal's streak and Balogun's confession

children.content: Spain showcases its biggest advantage over France, Lamine Yamal's hot streak goes on and Folarin Balogun opens up on his overturned red card. Here are today's quick hits.

articles.title: France falters at first real hurdle as superstars fail to shine

children.content: France's 2026 World Cup campaign is ended by a poor 2-0 defeat to Spain, as the much-hyped collection of star forwards fail to turn up for the biggest game yet.

image.altText: Desire Doue pulls his jersey over his face in disappointment

articles.title: Scottish traffic cone welcomed in Boston after World Cup friendship

children.content: The World Cup's obsession with Scotland fans and traffic cones continues as a witch's hat from Glasgow receives an official welcome from dignitaries in Boston.

image.altText: Massachusetts Governor Maura Healey poses wearing a traffic cone on her head next to two Scottish football fans.

articles.title: Spain soars into World Cup final with stunning upset over favourite France

children.content: Spain is through to the World Cup final thanks to a 2-0 win over France.

image.altText: Pedro Porro roars with delight

items.title: The new political parties hoping to deceive 'useful idiots' in Victoria

children.content: New political parties are seeking to exploit Victoria's anachronistic electoral system in the upper house to harvest voters' preferences and funnel them wherever they please.

image.altText: A Victorian Electoral Commission white-coloured ballot box

items.title: Bali-based businessman's death leaves Australians chasing money

children.content: Cameron Hughes, 39, died in a Balinese immigration detention centre last week under yet-to-be-confirmed circumstances. The ABC can reveal the Perth businessman left behind hundreds of thousands of dollars in unfinished business, with dozens of customers potentially out of pocket.

image.altText: Cameron speaking while standing in front of a car in his workshop.

items.title: Jewish student tells of her disgust at TikToks in Auschwitz car park

children.content: Paris Enten has told the antisemitism royal commission her experiences of Monash University, including during an overseas trip, caused her mental health to deteriorate.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair in a checked blazer and dark top stares off camera.

items.title: Buyer drops nearly $72m on one of world's most complete T-rex skeletons

children.content: One of the largest
Tyrannosaurus rex
skeletons discovered makes history as the most valuable dinosaur fossil bought at auction.

gallery.altText: A close-up of a tyrannosaurus rex skeleton mounted and displayed in the middle of an arid hilly landscape.

thumbnail.altText: A close-up of a tyrannosaurus rex skeleton mounted and displayed in the middle of an arid hilly landscape.

items.title: Brisbane teen was sent 'racist messages' before school stabbing, court hears

children.content: A 16-year-old accused of stabbing a fellow student at a Brisbane school had been sent racist messages in the months before, a court has heard.

image.altText: A sign reading "Richlands Magistrates Court".

items.title: Truck driver's sacking for $100k ice cream bungle was fair, finds commission

children.content: The Fair Work Commission rules a trucking company was within its rights to fire a driver after he set the temperature of his trailer full of ice cream more than 20 degrees too warm.

image.altText: A road train with the logo EHR in green.

items.title: The bittersweet reality of becoming an empty-nester

children.content: Experts say there can be unexpected grief for parents that comes from children leaving home.

gallery.altText: A middle aged woman sitting on a lounge chair with a coffee looking contemplative

thumbnail.altText: A middle aged woman sitting on a lounge chair with a coffee looking contemplative

items.title: Monet, Renoir, van Gogh masterpieces appear in two new blockbuster exhibitions

children.content: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

image.altText: Three paintings hung in a gallery with red walls.

items.title: Quiz: Dust off your passport and join us on a trip

children.content: Which of these peaks isn't in the Himalayas? And in what country would you find the island of Rapa Nui?

image.altText: Four Easter Island Moai silhouettes in a single file line on a platform in front of an orange setting sun and cloudy sky

items.title: Life at Antarctic research station: 24 minutes of sun after six weeks of darkness

children.content: Researchers spending winter at Australia's southernmost Antarctic research station gathered to celebrate the return of the sun following weeks of darkness.

gallery.altText: Person taking a photo of a sunrise in Antarctica
Group of people standing in the snow watching the sun rise.

thumbnail.altText: Person taking a photo of a sunrise in Antarctica
Group of people standing in the snow watching the sun rise.

items.title: Telstra slammed for putting 'burden of proof on consumers' for compensation

children.content: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

image.altText: Brady stands at a lectern, looking down

items.title: Batteries now cheaper than gas for peak demand power supply

children.content: Falling battery costs and a data centre-fuelled dash for gas have overturned decades of accepted wisdom: gas is no longer the cheapest way to meet peak power demand.

image.altText: Flames belch form two tall metal industrial chimneys.

items.title: Flight disruptions at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog

children.content: A number of flights have been delayed for take-off at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog while three others have been diverted to Melbourne.

gallery.altText: Three Qantas flights on tarmac at Adelaide Airport with fog around.
An image overlooking Frome Street with most parts of the street covered in fog.
Qantas flight in the sky flying to Melbourne due to fog.

thumbnail.altText: Three Qantas flights on tarmac at Adelaide Airport with fog around.
An image overlooking Frome Street with most parts of the street covered in fog.
Qantas flight in the sky flying to Melbourne due to fog.

items.title: Melbourne council bans e-bikes after six-year trial

children.content: Yarra Council says e-bike operator Lime refused to take responsibility for retrieving dumped bikes.

image.altText: Lime bikes close-up.

items.title: Kangaroo carrying 'little joey' shot with arrow, triggering search

children.content: Community members search for the injured animal, which was spooked off before wildlife volunteers could help.

image.altText: kangaroo with arrow through its back.

items.title: As Japan undergoes a defence shift, China warns it's 'becoming a powder keg'

children.content: While the world has been watching the rise of China, Japan has been in the midst of its biggest defence shift since World War II.

gallery.altText: Japanese naval officers look over a ship.

thumbnail.altText: Japanese naval officers look over a ship.

items.title: Child exploitation 'hiding in plain sight' in Thailand's sex industry

children.content: Thailand is struggling against a "hidden" world of child sexual exploitation that requires action from both the tourism industry and law enforcement agencies, according to NGOs.

image.altText: The black silhouette of a girl, with blurred lights in the background.

items.title: Writer E Jean Carroll collects $8m in Trump sex abuse and defamation case

children.content: Writer E Jean Carroll has collected nearly $US5.6 million ($8 million) from Donald Trump after a jury in 2023 found the US president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

image.altText: A woman stands in a street, wearing dark sunglasses, blue suit jacket, white shirt and blue and white scarf.

items.title: NRLW players eye off longer season and full-time salaries

children.content: A $5.3 billion broadcast deal, booming participation and new contracts on the horizon have NRLW stars hopeful their league can soar to new heights.

gallery.altText: A Cronulla NRLW player holds the ball close as she charges between two Brisbane tacklers.

thumbnail.altText: A Cronulla NRLW player holds the ball close as she charges between two Brisbane tacklers.

items.title: Suspected Widdecombe murder being treated as a targeted attack

children.content: After initially saying the alleged murder of British former politician Ann Widdecombe was not politically motivated, police now say they believe it was a targeted attack.

image.altText: An older woman wearing a light-blue rosette points as she speaks into a microphone in front of a Reform UK logo.

items.title: Crows defender Josh Worrell to face court over crash

children.content: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell has been reported by police and will face court over a traffic crash last week.

image.altText: crows players Josh worrell, text walker and others walking off field

items.title: Tourists turn away from NT as Kakadu travellers greeted by closure signs

children.content: It is known as the jewel in the Northern Territory's tourism crown, but visitors to Kakadu National Park are being greeted by closure signs blocking roads to key waterfall sites.

gallery.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.

thumbnail.altText: View of Twin Falls at Kakadu National Park.
